title: NIS2 Art.20/21 governance — Board approval, management training and risk acceptance evidence
industry: All sectors in NIS2 Annex I and II
exclusions: Does not interpret minutes of board meetings, sign legal attestations, file mandatory incident notices with competent authorities, or replace external legal opinions; it only reflects operational telemetry and workflow states present inside Splunk-accessible systems.
evidence: Scheduled saved search `UC-22.2.48 NIS2 Governance Evidence Correlation`, exported CSV/JSON snapshots in `index=audit_evidence`, and the `nis2-art20-governance` dashboard panel with RFC 3161 timestamped bundles.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.20 receives partial assurance because UC evidence proves the monitorable workflow or telemetry portion while legal approval and filings remain outside Splunk.
compliance.controlObjective: Produces NIS2 evidence for Art.20 using NIS2 Art.20/21 governance — Board approval, management training and risk acceptance evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search for NIS2 Art.20/21 governance — Board approval, management training and risk acceptance evidence, dashboard panel, and archived result export in index=audit_evidence for Art.20.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Fixture `sample-data/uc-22.2.48-fixture.json` loads `check_id=GOV-TRAIN-01` with `deadline_days=30`, injects `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task task_id=TASK9001 state=Open category=nis2_governance check_id=GOV-TRAIN-01 assigned_to=directorSmith`, `index=lms sourcetype=lms:completion person_id=P-441 course_code=NIS2-MGMT-1001 completion_date=2023-01-01`, and `index=grc sourcetype=grc:risk risk_id=RISK-441 risk_status=Accepted risk_acceptance_date=2024-06-01` so `training_stale=1`; the search returns one row with `evidence_id` populated.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same fixture but LMS posts `completion_date` within 45 days for `person_id=P-441`, ServiceNow shows `state=Closed` at `_time=now()-1d`, `risk_last_review` within policy, and `_audit` contains `savedsearch_name=NIS2_Board_Cyber_Posture_Monthly`; the correlation returns zero rows because every join indicates a closed loop.

dataSources: GRC register events in `index=grc sourcetype=grc:risk` expose extracted fields `risk_id`, `risk_owner`, `risk_status`, `last_review_date`, `risk_acceptance_date`, and `risk_level`. LMS completions arrive in `index=lms sourcetype=lms:completion` with `person_id`, `course_code`, `completion_date`, and `score`. ServiceNow governance tasks normalize to `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task` where `category=nis2_governance` and `task_id`, `state`, and `assigned_to` are required for SLA math. Board packet automation is corroborated via `index=_audit action=search` rows that record `savedsearch_name` executions (e.g., scheduled cyber posture PDFs). Reference lookups `nis2_management_body.csv` and `nis2_governance_checklist.csv` ship under the NIS2 knowledge app with Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) correlation optional and Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) mandatory for API-collected tasks.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) ≥7.0 for correlation dashboards; Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) ≥7.7 for `snow:task` modular inputs writing to `index=itsm`; optional Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055) if LMS SSO flows through Entra ID.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_governance_checklist.csv
| eval report_row=if(match(description,"(?i)posture|board packet|cyber report"),1,0)
| join type=left report_row [
    search index=_audit action=search info=succeeded savedsearch_name="NIS2_Board_Cyber_Posture_Monthly" earliest=-90d
    | stats latest(_time) as last_report_run
    | eval report_row=1
]
| join type=left check_id [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task category=nis2_governance earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(_time) as last_task_touch latest(state) as task_state latest(assigned_to) as assigned_to values(task_id) as task_ids by check_id
]
| join type=left person_id [
    search index=lms sourcetype=lms:completion earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as training_last_completion latest(score) as training_last_score by person_id
]
| join type=left risk_id [
    search index=grc sourcetype=grc:risk earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(risk_acceptance_date) as risk_acceptance_date latest(last_review_date) as risk_last_review latest(risk_status) as risk_status latest(risk_level) as risk_level latest(risk_owner) as risk_owner by risk_id
]
| lookup local=true nis2_management_body.csv person_id OUTPUT name, role, committee, appointment_date
| eval task_age_seconds=now()-last_task_touch
| eval training_stale=if(isnotnull(person_id) AND lower(required)="training", if(isnull(training_last_completion) OR relative_time(now(), "-365d")>strptime(training_last_completion, "%Y-%m-%d"),1,0),0)
| eval risk_review_stale=if(match(description,"(?i)risk acceptance") AND isnotnull(risk_id), if(isnull(risk_last_review) OR risk_status="Open",1,0),0)
| eval report_missing=if(report_row=1, if(isnull(last_report_run),1,0),0)
| eval task_overdue=if(isnotnull(task_age_seconds) AND task_age_seconds>deadline_days*86400 AND NOT task_state IN ("Closed","Resolved"),1,0)
| eval unowned_task=if(isnotnull(task_ids) AND (isnull(assigned_to) OR assigned_to=""),1,0)
| eval governance_gap=task_overdue OR training_stale OR risk_review_stale OR report_missing OR unowned_task
| where governance_gap=1
| eval evidence_id=sha256(check_id.if(isnotnull(person_id),person_id,"noperson").if(isnotnull(risk_id),risk_id,"norisk"))
| table check_id, description, person_id, name, role, committee, task_ids, task_state, assigned_to, training_last_completion, risk_id, risk_status, risk_acceptance_date, last_report_run, governance_gap, evidence_id
| sort - governance_gap, check_id
description: Starts from `nis2_governance_checklist.csv`, left-joins `snow:task` work tagged `category=nis2_governance`, LMS `lms:completion` history, GRC `grc:risk` acceptance timelines, `nis2_management_body.csv` roster metadata, and successful `index=_audit` executions of the monthly board cyber posture saved search—then keeps only rows where tasks exceed `deadline_days`, training is absent or stale, risk acceptance reviews never close, report runs never fired, or ownership fields are empty—emitting a deterministic `evidence_id` per gap.
value: Committees obtain a single, timestamped narrative that shows management discharged cyber governance duties in the systems of record instead of assembling binders manually before regulatory interviews; gaps route to named owners early so formal risk acceptance and board packets stay defensible, reducing fire drills and reputational surprises with supervisory authorities.
implementation: Publish the two CSV lookups under a governed Splunk app, ingest GRC/LMS/ITSM feeds, map `check_id` and `person_id` keys identically across ServiceNow tasks, LMS, and roster rows, then schedule the saved search weekly and archive JSON results to `index=audit_evidence` via summary indexing or HEC.
visualization: Executive governance swim-lane: checklist rows with red/amber tiles per `governance_gap` reason; drilldown links to ServiceNow `task_id`, LMS `course_code`, and GRC `risk_id`; 90-day trend of `last_report_run` for the board packet search.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for bespoke lookups; if you later map `grc:risk` into CIM Alerts, accelerate that model for tstats drilldowns only.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow on Splunkbase

knownFalsePositives: 1) **ServiceNow backlog grooming renames `check_id` values** — tasks exist but the join key no longer matches the checklist after a catalog import. Distinguish: `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:task category=nis2_governance | stats dc(check_id) by short_description` shows mismatched keys versus `| inputlookup nis2_governance_checklist.csv | stats dc(check_id)`. Suppress: maintain `nis2_governance_alias.csv` as a lookup mapping deprecated `check_id` values and apply `| lookup nis2_governance_alias.csv deprecated_check_id OUTPUTNEW check_id` before the join. 2) **LMS completion latency while course retakes are scheduled** — learners show `training_stale=1` during a planned retake window even though HR approved an extension. Distinguish: HR ticket references `person_id` with an approved extension reason. Suppress: time-bound exception rows in `nis2_exception_register.csv` keyed on `person_id` and `course_code` with `exception_valid_until`. 3) **Risk register row frozen pending board vote** — GRC keeps `risk_status=Pending_Board` longer than the SLA but the delay is intentional legal review. Distinguish: `index=grc sourcetype=grc:risk | search risk_id=RISK-7781` exposes `pending_board_vote=true`. Suppress: where `pending_board_vote="true"` filter the SPL via `| lookup nis2_exception_register.csv risk_id OUTPUT risk_exception | where isnull(risk_exception)`. 4) **Audit index retention pruned older `_audit` rows** so `last_report_run` appears null even though PDFs were delivered via email outside Splunk. Distinguish: mailbox journaling or SharePoint `evidence:export` rows still show the artifact. Suppress: ingest those artefacts into `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export` and add a secondary join on `report_batch_id`. 5) **Duplicate board members in `nis2_management_body.csv`** after HR merged accounts but Splunk retained legacy `person_id` values. Distinguish: `| inputlookup nis2_management_body.csv | stats dc(person_id) as ids values(person_id) as pids by email` shows duplicates. Suppress: deduplicate with `nis2_person_alias.csv` lookup before training joins.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, GRC policy approval workflow)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (lookups for governance register and board resolution tracking)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Technology Add-on**: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928) must be installed on search heads and indexers. Verify with `| rest /services/apps/local | search label="Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow" | table label version`.
• **Indexes**: `_audit`, `itsm`, `lms`, `grc` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `nis2_governance_checklist.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_governance_checklist.csv')`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.20`. The control objective is: "Produces NIS2 evidence for Art.20 using NIS2 Art.20/21 governance — Board approval, management training and risk acceptance evidence." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is performed by the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928), landing events in `_audit`, `itsm`, `lms`, `grc` under `snow:task`, `lms:completion`, `grc:risk`. REST-polled ticket tables produce 500-5000 records per sync per table; at 300s interval expect ~3-8 GB/month for a mid-size service desk.

Configure the input through Settings > Data Inputs > ServiceNow Service Account. A reference `inputs.conf` stanza looks like:

```ini
[snow://{instance_slug}]
url = https://<instance>.service-now.com
table = {table_name}
interval = 300
index = itsm
sourcetype = {sourcetype}
```

The following fields are authoritative for this detection once the TA parses them: `number, state, priority, assignment_group, opened_at, closed_at, sys_updated_on, caller_id, category, subcategory`. Verify they are extracted at search time with `index=_audit earliest=-15m | fieldsummary` before scheduling the detection.


One operational consideration shapes the deployment of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Publish the two CSV lookups under a governed Splunk app, ingest GRC/LMS/ITSM feeds, map `check_id` and `person_id` keys identically across ServiceNow tasks, LMS, and roster rows, then schedule the saved search weekly and archive JSON results to `index=audit_evidence` via summary indexing or HEC. Schedule cadence is a tradeoff between regulatory latency and search-head load; document the chosen cadence and the reasoning in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Art.20/21 governance — Board approval, management training and risk acceptance evidence," the aggregation is the evidence artifact itself — auditors will read these rows as the official record of NIS2 control activity.

This search correlates `index=_audit` (sourcetype=`snow:task`) on the outer side with `index=itsm` (sourcetype=`lms:completion`) on the inner side, joined on `report_row` across the last 90 days. `type=left` preserves all rows from the outer search; nulls in the inner side indicate missing data that may itself constitute a finding.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

Time-zone sensitivity: the `strptime(training_last_completion, "%Y-%m-%d")` conversion assumes the source timestamp is in the Splunk server's local time zone unless the format includes `%z`. If `training_last_completion` is emitted in UTC by the source system but Splunk's search head runs in a non-UTC zone, the resulting epoch will be off by the zone offset and the subsequent `where _time > training_last_completion_epoch` comparison will produce systematic false positives or negatives. Always validate by comparing `strftime(training_last_completion_epoch, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")` against a known-good reference event before promoting to production.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.48: NIS2 Art.20/21 governance — Board approval, management train]
cron_schedule = 0 3 1 * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -90d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.48,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=_audit sourcetype=snow:task earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `check_id`, `description`, `person_id`, `task_ids`, `task_state`, `assigned_to`, `training_last_completion`, `risk_id`, `risk_status`, `governance_gap`. Run:

```spl
index=_audit sourcetype=snow:task earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table check_id, description, person_id, task_ids, task_state, assigned_to, training_last_completion, risk_id
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.48: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Executive Governance Swim-Lane: Checklist Rows With Red/Amber Tiles Per `Governance Gap` Reason; Drilldown Links To Servicenow `Task Id` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Lms `Course Code` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — And Grc `Risk Id`; 90-Day Trend Of `Last Report Run` For The Board Packet Search (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: We stitch together who must approve cyber matters, who finished training, which risks were formally accepted, and whether routine board reports actually ran—then we flag what is late or missing so leaders can fix it before outsiders ask hard questions.
